title: Identify Library Type from Build Flags
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a program that reads library build command descriptions from stdin and reports whether each describes building a static library, a dynamic library, or neither.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 20), the number of commands to classify.
- Lines 2..N+1: each line is one of the following single-word tokens:
  - `STATIC` — represents a command that uses `ar rcs` to archive object files
  - `DYNAMIC` — represents a command that uses `-shared -fPIC` flags
  - `OTHER` — represents a command that does neither

## Output
For each line, print one of:
- `static library`
- `dynamic library`
- `other`

## Requirements
1. Read exactly N tokens.
2. Match `STATIC` → print `static library`, `DYNAMIC` → print `dynamic library`, `OTHER` → print `other`.
3. Each result on its own line, no trailing spaces.
4. N=1 with any token must work correctly.

title: Library Size Estimator Bug Fix
prompt_md:
## Goal
The program below is supposed to estimate the size of a statically linked executable by summing the sizes of all object files that are actually used (i.e., not marked unused). Find and fix the logic bug.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 50), the number of object files.
- Lines 2..N+1: two values per line: an integer `size` (bytes) and a string `status` (`used` or `unused`).

## Output
Print one line: `Total: X bytes` where X is the sum of sizes of all `used` object files.

## Requirements
1. Only sum object files with status `used`.
2. Output exactly `Total: X bytes` with no extra whitespace.
3. If no files are `used`, output `Total: 0 bytes`.

## Notes
- The program contains exactly one logic bug — find and fix it.
